001package applicationMain;
002        
003import java.sql.SQLException;
004import database.Database;
005import javafx.application.Application;
006import javafx.stage.Stage;
007import javafx.scene.control.Alert;
008import javafx.scene.control.Alert.AlertType;
009
010/*******
011 * <p> Title: FoundationsMain Class </p>
012 *
013 * <p> Description: This is the Main Class that launches the Foundations demonstration
014 * application.  This is a JavaFX application designed to serve as a foundation for the CSE360
015 * Individual Homework and Team Project assignments and demonstrate the use of the following:</p>
016 *
017 * <p>• The Singleton Design Pattern - The GUI uses the MVC Design Pattern, and each of the
018 *                      three components is instantiated once.  This requires special coding.  See this
019 *                      article for more insights: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Singleton_pattern</p>
020 * <p>• JavaDoc documentation</p>
021 * <p>• Internal documentation beyond JavaDoc with a focus on "why" (as well as "what" when it
022 *             might not be obvious)  The goal of the documentation is to help those who follow
023 *             you to benefit from your work without needing to do all the research and the
024 *             sometimes frustrating, if not painful, experimentation until you get it working.
025 *             This is especially true when the obvious way to do something does not work!</p>
026 *
027 * <p> On startup, the application tries to connect with the Foundations in-memory database.  If a
028 * connection to the database is currently active, an alert is displayed explaining the situation
029 * to the users and the application quits when the user acknowledges the alert.</p>
030 *
031 *  <p>If the connection is successful, a check is made to see if the database is empty. If so, this
032 * must be the first execution of the application and the person running the application is assumed
033 * to be an administrator.  That user is required to provide an Admin username and password before 
034 * anything else can happen.  Doing this eliminates a common weakness of "hard coded credentials".
035 * With that done, the admin can provide more details to the system (e.g., name and email address),
036 * and then proceed to do other admin activities.</p>
037 * 
038 *  <p>If the database is not empty, the system brings up the standard login page and requires an 
039 * existing user to log in. Once logged in, the user can perform whatever role(s) are set
040 * for that user.  This class's method stops as soon as the Graphical User Interface (GUI) for the
041 * one of the two options has been set up.  From that point forward, and actions are performed in
042 * reaction to the user engaging with widgets on the currently visible page.</p>
043 * 
044 *  <p>This application uses singletons and Model View Controller (MVC) View pattern to control the 
045 * use of memory by avoiding multiple copies of the same page.</p>
046 * 
047 *  <p>This application does not use the command line arguments *i.e., "String[] args"), but Java and
048 * JavaFX in Eclipse requires they be made available in the application's main method, even if they
049 * are not needed.</p>
050 *
051 * <p> Copyright: Lynn Robert Carter © 2025 </p>
052 *
053 *  <p>@author Lynn Robert Carter</p>
054 *
055 * @version 3.00        2025-08-17 Rewrite of this application for the Fall offering of CSE 360 and
056 * other ASU courses.
057 * @version 3.02        2025-12-17 Enhancements in support of Spring 2026
058 */
059
060public class FoundationsMain extends Application {
061        
062        /*-*******************************************************************************************
063
064        Attributes
065        
066        **********************************************************************************************/
067        
068        // These are the application values required by the user interface.  All the other classes
069        // access these constants to provide a uniform window size.     
070        public final static double WINDOW_WIDTH = 800;
071        public final static double WINDOW_HEIGHT = 600;
072
073        // These attributes establish the database and the fixed reference to it for the rest of the
074        // application so we do not need to keep passing the reference in parameters to the rest of the
075        // system for other methods that need it can access it.
076        public static Database database = new Database();
077    private Alert databaseInUse = new Alert(AlertType.INFORMATION);
078
079        public static int activeHomePage = 0;           // Which role's home page is currently active?
080                                                                                                // Role 0 is the admin role number
081        @Override
082        public void start(Stage theStage) {
083                
084                // Connect to the in-memory database
085                try {
086                        // Connect to the database
087                        database.connectToDatabase();
088                } catch (SQLException e) {
089                        // If the connection request fails, it usually means some other app is using it
090                        databaseInUse.setTitle("*** ERROR ***");
091                        databaseInUse.setHeaderText("Database Is Already Being Used");
092                        databaseInUse.setContentText("Please stop the other instance and try again!");
093                        databaseInUse.showAndWait();
094                        System.exit(0);
095                }
096                
097                // If the database is empty, no users have been established, so this user must be an admin
098                // user doing initial system startup activities and we need to set that admin's username
099                // and password using a special start you page.
100                if (database.isDatabaseEmpty()) {
101                        // This is a first use, so have the user set up the admin account
102                        guiFirstAdmin.ViewFirstAdmin.displayFirstAdmin(theStage);       
103                }
104                else
105                        // This is not a first use, so set up for the user to log in or create a new account
106                        guiUserLogin.ViewUserLogin.displayUserLogin(theStage);
107                
108                // With the JavaFX pages set up, this thread of the execution comes to an end.
109        }
110
111        /*******
112         * <p> Title: FoundationsMain main method that starts up JavaFX</p>
113         * 
114         * <p> Description: This main method does not perform any special function for this application
115         * beyond launching JavaFX.  Java and Eclipse require the application to be able to used the
116         * command line parameters, if needed.  This application does not use them.  If they are
117         * provided, the application will ignore them.</p>
118         * 
119         * @param String[] args   The array of command lines parameters.  These are not used.
120         */
121        public static void main(String[] args) {
122                launch(args);   // The launch method loads JavaFX and invokes its initialization.  When it
123                                                // is done, it calls the start method shown above.
124        }
125}
